checks.msg: too generic — trope_density (excluding declared anchors) above ceiling; these are unchosen clichés, not deliberate anchors. Re-voice toward specificity.
too much of this layer leans on declared anchors — conventions are a budget, not a loophole. Keep the anchors that carry the player; make the rest distinctive.
too much pure flavor — elements lack a mechanical hook; tie them to candidate mechanics
too counterintuitive — the fiction implies one thing but the mechanic does another, or the world-logic is confusing. Re-map so fiction and mechanic agree (legible, not generic).
economy too thin — seed >=4 INTERACTING systems (materials that transform/feed/sink into each other), not a flat list of resources (complexity_target).
ludic-hook ledger too sparse — surface >=12 distinct candidate mechanics, many of them interacting, so the GDD has deep material to draw on.

complete_msg: All world layers locked. WORLD.md + the ludic-hook ledger are ready to seed game-architect.
record_hint: run the layer's diverge→critique→synthesize micro-loop, then `record` it

any: planning/WORLD.md — this layer's section and every downstream layer's section
planning/ledger.json — the L7 element→mechanic table (regenerate after L7 rebuilds)
generated views of the world (e.g. world.html) — check planning/artifacts.json
the ENTIRE game-architect stage (design/) — the GDD was seeded by this world (its pinned WORLD.md hash will flag stale)
